layout_hierarchy.pattern: Executive summary strip at top. Two-column layout: charts left, KPI cards and commentary right.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Customer Deposits, Credit Score Distribution
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Customer Deposits over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Financial Navy (#1A237E) with gold (#FFD700) highlights and traffic-light status indicators
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Percentages: 1 decimal place (e.g., 23.4%). Large numbers: K/M suffix (e.g., 1.2M).
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

primary_interactions: Cross-filtering: clicking one chart filters all others
interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For intermediate users: balance simplicity with analytical depth.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With weekly updates, include a 'last updated' timestamp prominently.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts were selected based on the primary user task: trend analysis → line charts; comparison → bar charts; part-to-whole → pie/donut; single value → KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen to avoid cognitive overload. Group related KPIs visually using whitespace and section headers.
design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.

brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal logistics & supply chain systems. Updated monthly. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves C-Level Executives (CEO, CFO, COO) in the Logistics & Supply Chain sector. The primary goal is to track inventory levels and reorder points. Data is refreshed monthly, and the target users have intermediate data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: Stacked Bar Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Monitor trend over time' with Backorder Rate.
Donut Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Compare across categories' with Demand Forecast Accuracy.
Grouped Bar Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Track against target' with Cost per Shipment.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Single-page scrollable: Summary section → Trend section → Breakdown section → Details.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Backorder Rate, Demand Forecast Accuracy
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Backorder Rate over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Neutral Grey (#F5F5F5 background) with teal accents (#008080) and amber warnings (#FFA500)
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Percentages: 1 decimal place (e.g., 23.4%). Large numbers: K/M suffix (e.g., 1.2M).
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For intermediate users: balance simplicity with analytical depth.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With monthly updates, include a 'last updated' timestamp prominently.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts were selected based on the primary user task: trend analysis → line charts; comparison → bar charts; part-to-whole → pie/donut; single value → KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen to avoid cognitive overload. Group related KPIs visually using whitespace and section headers.
design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.

design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.

brief.title: Retail Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Analyze customer foot traffic and basket size
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal retail systems. Updated real-time. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Operations Team Leads in the Retail sector. The primary goal is to analyze customer foot traffic and basket size. Data is refreshed real-time, and the target users have intermediate data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: A funnel chart visualizes the conversion steps contributing to Same-Store Sales Growth.
A heatmap reveals patterns in Basket Size across two dimensions simultaneously.
A treemap shows the proportional contribution of sub-categories to total Loyalty Program Participation.
Bar charts enable easy comparison of Markdowns across discrete categories or time periods.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Full-width header with KPI cards. Below: tabbed sections for different analysis views.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Same-Store Sales Growth, Basket Size
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Same-Store Sales Growth over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Neutral Grey (#F5F5F5 background) with teal accents (#008080) and amber warnings (#FFA500)
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Integers for counts; 1-2 decimal places for averages; K/M suffix for large numbers.
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For intermediate users: balance simplicity with analytical depth.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With real-time updates, use auto-refresh and highlight recent changes.
